title: macOS: GPG error when pulling RH operator index (OperatorHub) images
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I'll preface this by stating that I'm a Fedora user and not too familiar with macOS, and specifically how podman is wired to run on a mac. But regardless, colleagues of mine who use mac (and which I've confirmed on a 10th-gen Intel-based macbook air) have reported an inability to pull the following images w/podman:

registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:<v4.x>
registry.redhat.io/redhat/redhat-marketplace-index:<v4.x>

Pulling these images (once the proper GPG key is setup) on Fedora works just fine. Performing the same `podman pull` command on macOS yields a GPG error. I'm not sure if podman respects the `/usr/local/containers/policy.json` file, but I've tried installing `skopeo` via homebrew in order to obtain that file, and editing it to reference the ISV Container Signing Key for the pull specs listed above.

Nothing seems to resolve the GPG error.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install the latest podman on macOS [via homebrew](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#macos):

```
$ brew install podman
```

2. Attempt to pull any of the operator index images, used to populate OperatorHub on OpenShift:

```
$ podman pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.11
```

3. Observe output.

**Describe the results you received:**

When performing the above steps, I receive the following GPG related error:

```
Trying to pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.11...
Error: Source image rejected: Invalid GPG signature: gpgme.Signature{Summary:128, Fingerprint:"1AC4971355A34A82", Status:gpgme.Error{err:0x9}, Timestamp:time.Date(2022, time.August, 9, 8, 13, 51, 0, time.Local), ExpTimestamp:time.Date(1969, time.December, 31, 17, 0, 0, 0, time.Local), WrongKeyUsage:false, PKATrust:0x0, ChainModel:false, Validity:0, ValidityReason:error(nil), PubkeyAlgo:1, HashAlgo:8}
ERROR: Failed to launch index container
Exiting...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect `podman` to pull the image as it would on other platforms.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I have a feeling that the VM used by podman-machine is lacking the ISV-Container-Signing-Key ([provided by Red Hat](https://www.redhat.com/security/data/55A34A82.txt)) and having the proper config for the affected image repos in `/etc/containers/policy.json` in the virtual machine. I'm not versed on how to access or configure the podman VM in such case.

I'm not sure if the the `policy.json` file or any GPG related libraries are at all referenced from the macOS host environment, or if these are contained and utilized entirely within the podman-machine VM.

**Output of `podman version`:**
The below output is from the macbook air:

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.5

Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 10:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ brew list podman
/usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.2.0/bin/podman
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

macOS Catalina 10.15.7

comments.body:
Closing, since a colleague shared that they'd gotten this to work.

For anyone else who may have run into this using podman and enforcing GPG signatures on images (on macOS), here's the workaround [sic]:

"you can do `podman machine ssh`, then do `sudo su -`, and edit `/etc/containers/policy.json` to change the entry type for registry.redhat.io from `"type": "signedby"` to `"type": "insecureAcceptAnything"`, delete the trailing comma and the `keyType` and `keyPath` lines, and exit out of the VM. Then the podman pull command will work."
